response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and event-driven architecture experience, but the role demands deep, sustained C++ expertise as its core technical requirement. His C++ exposure is limited to a brief stint at National Instruments (7 months, 2014–2015) and a university thesis project — neither of which reflects the 8–12 years of production-grade C++/STL development this position requires. He has no documented experience with IEC 62443 secure development lifecycles, industrial automation protocols (OPC UA, Modbus, MQTT), or IoT platform engineering, which are central to the role. His distributed-systems and high-throughput background at Sociomantic shows relevant engineering depth, but it does not compensate for the missing domain-specific and language-specific foundations.
strengths: Demonstrated high-performance, low-latency distributed systems experience (Sociomantic: millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, fiber-based concurrency)
Strong event-driven and message-broker architecture background through GISCollective and microservices work
Active mentoring and knowledge-sharing culture (workshops, Code Katas, Selenium courses, conference talks)
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and over a decade old — primary production stack is TypeScript/DLang/Node.js, not C++/STL
No industrial cybersecurity knowledge (IEC 62443, SDL) and no exposure to industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, MQTT, IEC 61850)
No IoT or industrial automation domain background; cloud experience is AWS-centric with no Azure exposure
